title: Exercise Physiologists
description: Assess, plan, or implement fitness programs that include exercise or physical activities such as those designed to improve cardiorespiratory function, body composition, muscular strength, muscular endurance, or flexibility.

tasks.text: Develop exercise programs to improve participant strength, flexibility, endurance, or circulatory functioning, in accordance with exercise science standards, regulatory requirements, and credentialing requirements.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Provide emergency or other appropriate medical care to participants with symptoms or signs of physical distress.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Demonstrate correct use of exercise equipment or performance of exercise routines.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Recommend methods to increase lifestyle physical activity.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Interpret exercise program participant data to evaluate progress or identify needed program changes.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Prescribe individualized exercise programs, specifying equipment, such as treadmill, exercise bicycle, ergometers, or perceptual goggles.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Provide clinical oversight of exercise for participants at all risk levels.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Explain exercise program or physiological testing procedures to participants.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Interview participants to obtain medical history or assess participant goals.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Assess physical performance requirements to aid in the development of individualized recovery or rehabilitation exercise programs.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Teach behavior modification classes related to topics such as stress management or weight control.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Conduct stress tests, using electrocardiograph (EKG) machines.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Measure oxygen consumption or lung functioning, using spirometers.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Educate athletes or coaches on techniques to improve athletic performance, such as heart rate monitoring, recovery techniques, hydration strategies, or training limits.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Evaluate staff performance in leading group exercise or conducting diagnostic tests.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Teach group exercise for low-, medium-, or high-risk clients to improve participant strength, flexibility, endurance, or circulatory functioning.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Calibrate exercise or testing equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Teach courses or seminars related to exercise or diet for patients, athletes, or community groups.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Mentor or train staff to lead group exercise.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Measure amount of body fat, using such equipment as hydrostatic scale, skinfold calipers, or tape measures.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Perform routine laboratory tests of blood samples for cholesterol level or glucose tolerance.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Supervise maintenance of exercise or exercise testing equipment.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Present exercise knowledge, program information, or research study findings at professional meetings or conferences.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Order or recommend diagnostic procedures, such as stress tests, drug screenings, or urinary tests.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Plan or conduct exercise physiology research projects.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
